summary: The user sought recommendations for fantasy novels similar to Patrick Rothfuss' style after finishing "The Name of the Wind." The assistant provided a list of ten series and detailed information about Scott Lynch's "The Gentlemen Bastards," including its setting, plot, writing style, and historical inspirations.
facts: The user finished reading "The Name of the Wind" by Patrick Rothfuss. The user is interested in fantasy novels similar to Rothfuss' style. The user has heard of Joe Abercrombie and Brandon Sanderson but not the other authors recommended. The user is interested in exploring more about Scott Lynch's "The Gentlemen Bastards" series. The main city in "The Gentlemen Bastards" series, Camorr, is inspired by Renaissance-era Venice. The first book in the series is titled "The Lies of Locke Lamora." The plot of "The Lies of Locke Lamora" follows a group of con artists led by Locke Lamora in the underworld of Camorr. The user is interested in the Elder Glass and the ancient civilizations in the world of the Gentlemen Bastards. The Bondsmagi are a powerful group of magic-users in the Gentlemen Bastards series. The Bondsmagi have a complex relationship with the Gentlemen Bastards, often viewing them as reckless.
